intro.meta: Discover the Go Green School approach
intro.title: Practical programs that teach sustainability with purpose.
intro.description: Our curriculum blends hands-on learning with real-world impact. Students gain confidence through activities that support recycling, energy efficiency, and environmental stewardship, while the school builds a stronger culture of eco-awareness.

why.title: Why this matters
why.text: Students learn by doing, turning awareness into measurable change across the school and community.

what.title: What you will find
what.text: Structured programs for waste management, green gardening, and energy-saving habits with clear student outcomes.

visi_misi.title: Vision & Mission
visi_misi.visi_content: To be a pioneering school in sustainable education that produces an environmentally conscious generation with strong character, ready to face future challenges.
misi_items: Integrate environmental education into all subjects
Implement eco-friendly practices in all school activities
Develop critical and creative thinking skills through environmental projects
Build partnerships with communities for broader environmental impact
Foster green entrepreneurship spirit in every student

program_info.title: Guiding the Go Green School Program
program_info.subtitle: This page brings together the full program story: purpose, structure, educational value, practical actions, and the steps schools follow to make sustainability part of daily life.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.text: Go Green School runs a learning program built around environmental stewardship and meaningful action. It creates a framework where students and teachers collaborate to make waste reduction, energy efficiency, and green living part of daily routines.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: This program combines hands-on projects, school-wide campaigns, and community involvement. It is designed to be accessible for every school, using simple practices that students can carry home and share with their families.

sections.title: Explanation
sections.text: The core idea is that schools are powerful places for change. When students practice sustainable habits in class, they learn responsibility, empathy, and systems thinking. The program turns abstract environmental topics into concrete activities that feel relevant and rewarding.

sections.title: Information
sections.text: Go Green School covers several practical areas that schools can implement step by step. Each element is selected to support learning outcomes and environmental impact together.
items: Waste sorting and recycling programs that teach students the value of materials.
Energy-saving habits, such as turning off lights, monitoring power use, and using efficient devices.
School gardens and planting activities that deepen understanding of nature and nutrition.
Collaboration between students, teachers, and parents to make green habits part of everyday life.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.text: A successful Go Green School program starts with awareness and moves into action. Schools plan clear steps, assign roles, measure progress, and reflect on results so every year’s efforts become stronger.
items: Begin with learning sessions and simple eco-challenges for students.
Create student teams to manage waste, energy, and gardening activities.
Monitor results with easy measures, then celebrate progress and share success stories.
Use school events and community outreach to spread awareness beyond the campus.
